title: mnemion tenellum
parts: Mnemion tenellum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, which are rich in flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, and also contain terpenoids like beta-sitosterol. The flowers of the plant are known to contain a variety of alkaloids, including berberine and palmitic acid, as well as saponins like oleanolic acid. The roots of Mnemion tenellum contain sesquiterpenes, including germacranolide and guaiane derivatives, which have shown potential in traditional medicine for various health benefits. Additionally, the plant's stems contain tannins and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Mnemion tenellum can have side effects if used improperly, such as rapid heartbeat, dizziness, and nausea due to its ability to stimulate the nervous system. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can lead to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues. In severe cases, improper use of Mnemion tenellum may cause seizures, convulsions, or even coma due to its powerful neuroactive properties. Additionally, long-term misuse of this plant can also lead to vitamin deficiencies and gastrointestinal problems.
